text.title: LoraConfig Parameters and Trainable Parameter Verification
text.summary: LoraConfig construction with rank, alpha, dropout, and target_modules; trainable parameter count check; rank selection guide by task type.

rule.statement: Print trainable parameter count immediately after get_peft_model() — if it shows 0, stop and fix target_modules before starting training.
rule.rationale: Wrong target_modules names cause 0 trainable parameters; training proceeds silently for hours producing a model identical to the base model.

example.code:
from peft import LoraConfig, get_peft_model, TaskType
lora_config = LoraConfig( r=16, lora_alpha=16, target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj"], lora_dropout=0.05, bias="none", task_type=TaskType.CAUSAL_LM, ) model = get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
def check_lora_params(model) -> dict: trainable = sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters() if p.requires_grad) total = sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters()) if trainable == 0: raise ValueError("No trainable params — check target_modules names") return {"trainable": trainable, "total": total, "pct": f"{100 * trainable / total:.4f}%"}
stats = check_lora_params(model) print(stats) # Expected: trainable% between 0.01% and 2%
example.note: Raises immediately if target_modules are wrong, preventing hours of wasted GPU time.

rule.statement: Set lora_alpha equal to lora_r as a default; only deviate after observing training dynamics.
rule.rationale: The scaling factor lora_alpha/r controls effective learning rate for the adapter; equal values provide a neutral baseline that works for most tasks.

list.item: rank 8: minimal memory, simple extraction or classification tasks
rank 16: general-purpose default for instruction following and style adaptation
rank 32: complex tasks requiring nuanced behavioral changes
rank 64+: rarely justified; use only when rank 32 validation loss is still plateauing with >5k examples

rule.statement: Always set tokenizer.pad_token = tokenizer.eos_token before training if the tokenizer has no pad token.
rule.rationale: Missing pad token causes silent data corruption in batched training; the model trains on misaligned sequences.

rule.statement: Save the tokenizer alongside the adapter: tokenizer.save_pretrained(output_dir) — never save the adapter alone.
rule.rationale: An adapter saved without the tokenizer cannot be reproduced or shared; model IDs without matching tokenizers are not deployable.
